quick_facts.label: Training context
quick_facts.value: Official Ministry of Defence accounting guidance describes a probationary attachment to airborne forces for personnel serving in Parachute units and formations; it is linked to subsequent attendance at a Basic Parachute Course and parachute-duty qualification.

quick_facts.label: Documented minimum probation attachment
quick_facts.value: For the parachute-pay rules cited in the official guidance, the relevant probationary attachment is one month. This should not be treated as a published duration for every Para Regiment or Para (Special Forces) selection pathway, because the public source does not set out a complete selection syllabus or universal probation length.

quick_facts.label: Institutional destination
quick_facts.value: The Ministry of Defence publicly identifies both conventional Parachute Regiment battalions and Parachute Regiment (Special Forces) battalions, confirming that the Regiment includes a special-forces stream.

quick_facts.label: Public-information limitation
quick_facts.value: The official sources located describe probationary attachment, parachute-course qualification, duty status and allowances, but do not publish a public civilian application form, a complete probation test battery, a pass rate, or universal age/service thresholds for transfer volunteers.

sections.heading: Eligibility: a serving-personnel pathway, not a civilian course
paragraphs: This node should be understood as an internal Indian Army pathway for serving personnel rather than a standalone civilian education programme. The official material publicly available refers to personnel serving in Parachute units and airborne formations, their probationary attachment, parachute-course qualification and parachute duties; it does not publish a general public enrolment route for the process.

The public record is insufficient to state a universal age limit, minimum prior service, medical standard, vacancy rule, or a guaranteed right for every Sepoy to volunteer. Those details should therefore be obtained through the soldier’s chain of command and current unit/Army orders rather than inferred from unofficial online claims.
bullets: A candidate must already be within the service system relevant to the posting or attachment; this is not presented in the cited government material as direct civilian recruitment.
Probationary attachment is associated with a parachute formation/airborne-force context and with the Basic Parachute Course.

Eligibility, attachment and any waiver are administratively controlled; the officer guidance specifically refers to waivers by administrative authorities where the prescribed probation period was not completed.

sections.heading: Duration: what official public sources establish
paragraphs: The clearest official, publicly accessible duration is a one-month probationary attachment used in the rules governing parachute allowance. For personnel serving in Parachute units and airborne formations, the guidance ties eligibility for parachute pay after Basic Parachute Course qualification to one month from the start of probationary attachment, subject to the stated timing rules.

That one-month figure is a documented administrative/pay condition. It is not adequate evidence for claiming that all Para Regiment or Para (Special Forces) volunteer selection and continuation training lasts exactly one month; the detailed operational selection timeline is not published in the official sources reviewed.
bullets: Published qualification-related probationary attachment: one month.
The source links the attachment with the Basic Parachute Course and applies timing conditions when the gap before the course exceeds three months.
For continuation of Para pay, JCOs/other ranks are required to attend a yearly parachute refresher course that includes at least two jumps, subject to listed exemptions.

sections.heading: Training content supported by public sources
paragraphs: The public government documentation supports a narrow but important training sequence: probationary attachment in an airborne setting, a Basic Parachute Course, qualification as a parachutist, and continuing refresher activity. It does not publish the physical tests, field exercises, navigation tasks, weapons standards, sleep-deprivation regime, or selection scoring often described in unofficial accounts.

After qualification, continued parachute-duty status is connected to refresher training. The cited manual specifies a refresher course in each training year, including a minimum of two jumps, for continuation of Para pay for JCOs and other ranks, with provisions for operational jumps and limited exemptions.
bullets: Probationary attachment to airborne forces or a parachute formation.
Qualification for parachute duties and subsequent parachute descents where required by normal duties.
Annual refresher requirement for the applicable pay entitlement: a parachute refresher course including at least two jumps.

sections.heading: Admission and administrative control
paragraphs: There is no verified public admissions portal or civilian application deadline for this node. The available official sources instead show an internal administrative process: allowances are authorised through Part II Orders, the relevant appointment must be authorised in the unit/formation establishment, and commander or administrative-authority decisions can be relevant where a course is not completed or probationary attachment is waived.

For a serving Sepoy, the practical route is consequently unit-led: raise the intention through the chain of command and rely on current Army instructions for vacancies, medical categorisation, approvals, attachment and reporting. This is an operational inference from the internal-order structure in the official material, not a claim that every request must be accepted.
bullets: No publicly verified civilian application form or published annual deadline was found in the official sources reviewed.
Part II Orders are the documented authority used for the relevant Para allowance administration.
The official guidance requires the person to be employed on parachute duties and to hold an appointment authorised by the relevant establishment for the allowance context.

sections.heading: What follows successful parachute qualification
paragraphs: The publicly documented immediate progression is qualification at the Basic Parachute Course and employment on parachute duties in an authorised appointment. Personnel who remain in the applicable parachute-duty framework may continue to receive parachute pay while they meet the refresher requirements and remain medically fit for jumping, under the rules cited.

The Regiment also contains identified Parachute Regiment (Special Forces) battalions. However, the official sources reviewed do not establish that completing the one-month probationary attachment or Basic Parachute Course automatically transfers a soldier into a Para (Special Forces) battalion. Such posting or selection should be treated as subject to separate, current Army authority and requirements.
bullets: Basic Parachute Course qualification and parachute-duty employment are the verified immediate outcomes in the official guidance.
Ongoing refresher participation is relevant to continued Para pay in the cited rules.
Parachute Regiment (Special Forces) battalions exist, but no automatic progression rule from basic parachute qualification to that stream is publicly substantiated here.
